sanskrit:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया |
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ||
transliteration:
puruṣaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha bhaktyā labhyas tv ananyayā |
yasyāntaḥsthāni bhūtāni yena sarvam idaṁ tatam ||
literal_meaning: The Supreme Person, who is higher than all, O Pārtha, is attainable by unalloyed devotion; within whom all beings dwell, and by whom all this is pervaded.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that the highest existential ground—an unbreakable, anxiety-free center—can be reached only through single-pointed, non-contingent love. This center is not a distant heaven but the very field in which every thought, feeling, and atom already lives.
psychological_state: Secure-base activation: the nervous system shifts from anxious scanning to felt-safety when the object of devotion is experienced as both container and content of experience.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver, convinced they must fix everything alone, suddenly senses that their fatigue is held inside a larger, benevolent awareness; the compulsive doing relaxes into effortless being.
anchor_text: Love the Supreme with single-pointed devotion and find the unshakable center within all.
